title: Integrity Preservation Cannot Be Reconstructed
scenario: A clinical action depends on preserved integrity, but available evidence cannot reconstruct whether integrity was preserved at execution time.
initial_state: Integrity preservation was assumed during authorization.
state_transition: Integrity evidence becomes missing, inconsistent, or unrecoverable.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Execution requires reconstructable evidence that clinical integrity remains preserved.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because integrity preservation cannot be reconstructed.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when integrity preservation cannot be reconstructed.
clinical_action: Execute treatment with unreconstructable integrity preservation
